what_it_does: Defines JSX components for a live real-estate brochure preview that displays completion states, placeholder content, missing required fields, static brochure copy, feature stats, color swatches, and output format indicators based on a filled prop.
observations: The file defines BrochureSection and OutputPreview as function components.
BrochureSection maps done, pending, and gap states to visual tags and human-readable state labels.
OutputPreview renders a brochure preview for Tower East in Sydney CBD with a hero, body sections, and footer.
The filled prop controls title, brand palette, feature stats, description, price, agent contact, and finalize state.
Pending sections show muted or skeleton placeholder UI, while missing price and agent fields use error-colored text.
Static brochure content includes apartment stats, description text, source label, and export format labels.
The component is exposed globally through window.OutputPreview.
standouts: The preview is organized as a brochure with explicit section completion states.
Missing required data is surfaced separately from drafting data through the gap state.
The file mixes reusable section structure with hard-coded real-estate brochure content.
conventions: Uses conditional rendering and nested ternaries for state-dependent UI.
Uses CSS class names plus inline style objects.
Uses CSS custom properties for colors and fonts.
Uses array mapping to render palette swatches.
Exports by assigning the component to window rather than using an ES module export.
concerns: Several presentation details are embedded as inline styles.
Brochure copy, location, palette values, feature stats, and source text are hard-coded.
The filled object shape is assumed without defaults or validation.
BrochureSection silently renders no tag or state label for unrecognized states.
notes: This file is a global JSX brochure preview widget driven by a simple filled-state object.
questions: Is window.OutputPreview required by the surrounding runtime?
Are done, pending, and gap the complete set of allowed section states?
Should the hard-coded Tower East content be sample data or production preview content?
What shape and defaults are expected for the filled prop?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it renders a brochure preview that updates from completion state.
intention_signals: It seems intended to show users a brochure being assembled in real time, distinguishing filled content, draft placeholders, and missing required information before final output formats become active.
novelty: A compact brochure mockup combines skeleton drafting states, missing-field alerts, palette swatches, real-estate stats, and export readiness indicators in one file-local preview component.
contribution: This file contributes a state-to-preview layer for a brochure workflow, preserving completion labels, placeholder rendering, missing-field visibility, static real-estate preview content, palette/stat display, and format readiness cues.
summary_for_embedding: OutputPreview.jsx defines two JSX functions that render a real-estate brochure preview with state-labeled sections, skeleton placeholders, missing-field markers, static sample content, and a global window export.
intention_for_embedding: It was for showing a live auto-saving brochure output as source fields become filled, draft, missing, or ready to finalize.

symbols.does: Renders a labeled brochure section with a visual status tag and child content.
symbols.description: BrochureSection accepts label, state, and children, converts the state into a tag and state label, and wraps the section content in a brochure section container. It applies a pending class only for pending state and recognizes done, pending, and gap as explicit states.

symbols.does: Renders the complete live brochure preview using the filled prop to choose completed, drafting, missing, and finalized UI states.
symbols.description: OutputPreview builds the preview shell, hero, brochure body, and footer for a Tower East apartment brochure. It passes state values into BrochureSection, conditionally renders real values or placeholders, shows missing price and agent fields in an error color, and toggles PDF, DOC, and HTML format indicators from filled.finalize.
